steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content: The quadratic $5x^2-9x-3=0$ does not factor cleanly with integer factors, so use the quadratic formula with $a=5$, $b=-9$, and $c=-3$: $$x=\frac{9\pm\sqrt{(-9)^2-4(5)(-3)}}{2(5)}=\frac{9\pm\sqrt{141}}{10}.$$

steps.title: Identify the greatest solution
steps.content: The larger root takes the $+$ sign: $\tfrac{9}{10}+\tfrac{\sqrt{141}}{10}$. That matches Choice B — $\frac{9}{10} + \frac{\sqrt{141}}{10}$.
